activities_projects.title: Investigating Thermal Conductivity of Various Metals
activities_projects.objective: Compare heat conduction rates of metals quantitatively
steps: Heat one end of each metal rod over the flame
Place wax drops at the other end
Record time for wax to melt
Measure temperature along rod every 30 seconds
Analyze and rank metals by thermal conductivity
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Quantitative understanding of conduction in different metals
activities_projects.evaluation: Data accuracy, analysis, and reasoning (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Modeling Convection Currents
activities_projects.objective: Visualize and explain convection in fluids
steps: Fill beaker with water and heat from one side
Add food color at multiple points
Observe convection currents forming and moving
Sketch patterns and explain heat movement
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands movement of heat by convection and identifies current patterns
activities_projects.evaluation: Observation detail and explanation accuracy (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Comparing Radiation Absorption by Different Materials
activities_projects.objective: Investigate how color, texture, and surface affect heat absorption
materials: Black, white, silver, and shiny metal sheets
steps: Place thermometers on each material under sunlight/heat lamp
Record temperature every 5 minutes for 30 minutes
Analyze which materials absorb and retain heat most
Discuss reasons for differences
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Relates material properties to radiation absorption
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct analysis and scientific reasoning (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Designing a Highly Efficient Insulator
activities_projects.objective: Apply knowledge of heat transfer to create an effective insulator
steps: Wrap one cup with combination of materials to minimize heat loss
Leave the other cup unwrapped as control
Pour equal hot water in both
Record temperature every 5 minutes for 30 minutes
Evaluate effectiveness and suggest improvements
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies conduction and insulation principles to real-life design

activities_projects.title: Comparing Heat Transfer in Air vs Water vs Solid
activities_projects.objective: Investigate rate of heat transfer in different mediums
steps: Apply equal heat to water, air, and metal
Measure temperature change at set intervals
Compare conduction and convection effects
Explain differences using scientific principles
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Compares conduction and convection across states of matter
activities_projects.evaluation: Data collection and scientific explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Investigating Urban Heat Effect
activities_projects.objective: Observe how surface types affect heat in environment
materials: Black asphalt sheet, concrete slab, grassy area
steps: Measure surface temperatures at the same time in sunlight
Record temperatures every 10 minutes for 30 minutes
Compare heat retention on different surfaces
Explain findings using heat transfer concepts
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies heat transfer understanding to environmental science
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of data and explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Measuring Heat Retention of Liquids in Containers
activities_projects.objective: Quantify effect of container type and color on heat retention
materials: Black, white, metal, and glass cups
steps: Pour equal hot water into all cups
Measure temperature at 5-minute intervals for 30 minutes
Analyze which combination retains heat best
Draw conclusions about color and material effect
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands practical application of radiation and conduction

activities_projects.title: Simulating Convection in Air Currents
activities_projects.objective: Observe and record convection patterns in air
steps: Light incense stick inside glass box
Heat one side of the box
Observe smoke movement forming convection currents
Record and sketch patterns
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Visualizes air convection and relates to natural phenomena
activities_projects.evaluation: Sketch clarity and scientific explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Design Your Own Thermos Experiment
activities_projects.objective: Test creative ways to minimize heat loss
materials: Cups, insulating materials (cotton, foil, foam, bubble wrap)
steps: Design cup insulation using multiple materials
Measure temperature change over 30 minutes
Compare with control cup
Document design choices and reasoning
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies heat transfer concepts in real-life design and evaluation
activities_projects.evaluation: Effectiveness and innovation of design (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Create a Data-Based Quiz on Heat Transfer
steps: Prepare five quantitative questions based on experiments
Include reasoning-based multiple choice or short answer questions
Exchange with a friend and solve
Discuss answers and reasoning
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Integrates experimentation, data analysis, and conceptual understanding
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of questions, correctness, and reasoning (10 marks)
